operating_state: Total revenue increased to $6.6 billion in Q3 2025 from $6.4 billion prior quarter, driven by higher domestic oil prices and increased sales volumes. Oil and gas segment earnings rose significantly with average daily sales volumes increasing to 1,468 Mboe from 1,397 Mboe due to enhanced Midland Basin production from current year drilling programs. Nine-month revenue declined year-over-year primarily due to lower crude oil prices despite higher volumes from full-year CrownRock asset integration.
cost_structure: Depreciation, depletion and amortization increased to $5.9 billion for nine months from $5.4 billion prior year, reflecting full-year impact of CrownRock assets. Chemical segment margins compressed due to lower realized prices across most product lines combined with higher raw material and energy costs. Operating expenses increased due to expanded low-carbon ventures business activities, partially offset by favorable raw material costs in chemicals.
material_events: Occidental entered definitive agreement in October 2025 to sell OxyChem to Berkshire Hathaway for $9.7 billion in all-cash transaction, with majority of after-tax proceeds targeted for debt reduction. Company completed warrant exercise in April 2025, issuing 41.9 million shares for approximately $890 million proceeds. Occidental repaid $3.6 billion in debt during nine months including senior notes and term loan maturities.
macro_exposures: President Trump announced sweeping tariff policy in April 2025 imposing baseline tariffs on majority of imports with higher reciprocal rates for certain nations, though many rates have been paused, modified or reinstated amid ongoing litigation. Tariff implementation could increase supplier costs and impact macroeconomic demand and product pricing. Oil price volatility continues driven by geopolitical risks and evolving macro-economic environment's impact on energy demand.
forward_outlook: Management prioritizes excess cash flow and OxyChem divestiture proceeds for deleveraging to reduce principal debt below $15 billion target. Company maintains focus on production base preservation, sustainable dividend growth, and enhanced asset base investments in cash-generative oil and gas business. Occidental advances integrated CO2, power and midstream technologies for differentiated resource recovery while developing carbon management solutions.
uncertainty_profile: OxyChem transaction completion subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions with termination rights if closing not achieved by March 30, 2026. Company retains environmental liabilities for OxyChem legacy sites and faces post-closing indemnification obligations. Zero Coupon bonds can be put to Occidental in October 2026 requiring $401 million payment if exercised in whole.
